description: Fix stale UI state bugs in the Chrome extension: activeTools Set that grows unboundedly causing permanent spinning indicators, and confirmation badge count that never clears when the side panel is closed during confirmation timeout.

userStories.title: Prune stale activeTools entries on plugin removal and add invocation timeout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to stop showing spinning indicators for tools that are no longer executing, so that the UI accurately reflects the current state.
acceptanceCriteria: When plugins.changed notification arrives and loadPlugins refreshes the plugin list, activeTools entries whose plugin prefix no longer matches any loaded plugin are removed
When a tool.invocationStart adds an entry to activeTools, a 5-minute timeout is set that auto-removes the entry if tool.invocationEnd never arrives
When tool.invocationEnd arrives, the corresponding timeout is cleared alongside removing the activeTools entry
When the WebSocket disconnects (existing behavior), all timeouts are cleared alongside the activeTools Set reset
userStories.notes:
Two changes needed:

1. **Prune on plugin removal** — In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx`, in the `plugins.changed` handler (around line 135-139), after `loadPlugins()` resolves, call `setActiveTools(prev => { const next = new Set<string>(); for (const key of prev) { if (updatedPlugins.some(p => key.startsWith(p.name + ':'))) next.add(key); } return next; })`. The challenge is that `loadPlugins` is async and updates state — one approach is to have `loadPlugins` return the updated plugin list, or to add the pruning inside the `loadPlugins` `.then()` callback using the fetched result.

2. **Invocation timeout** — In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useServerNotifications.ts`, add a `useRef<Map<string, ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>>` for invocation timeouts. In the `tool.invocationStart` handler (line 97-107), after adding to `activeTools`, set a 5-minute timeout that removes the key. In the `tool.invocationEnd` handler (line 109-123), clear the timeout. In the cleanup effect, clear all invocation timeouts. The timeout constant (5 minutes) should go in `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/constants.ts`.

The `setActiveTools` dispatcher is already passed to `useServerNotifications`, so no new props needed. Follow the existing pattern of `timeoutIds` ref used for confirmation timeouts in the same file.

userStories.title: Clear stale confirmation badge when side panel is closed during timeout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the extension badge to not show stale confirmation counts when confirmations time out while the side panel is closed, so that the badge accurately reflects pending actions.
acceptanceCriteria: When a confirmation.request arrives and the side panel is not open, the badge is cleared after the confirmation's timeoutMs expires (server-side timeout means the confirmation is no longer actionable)
The background service worker tracks confirmation timeouts independently of the side panel React component
When the side panel opens and processes a confirmation, the background timeout is cleared to avoid double-clearing the badge
When sp:confirmationResponse or sp:confirmationTimeout arrives from the side panel, any background-side timeout for that confirmation is cleared
When WebSocket disconnects, all background-side confirmation timeouts are cleared alongside clearAllConfirmationBadges()
userStories.notes:
The root cause: `confirmation-badge.ts` increments `pendingConfirmationCount` on every `confirmation.request` but only decrements it via `clearConfirmationBadge()` which is called from `sp:confirmationResponse` and `sp:confirmationTimeout` — both sent by the side panel React component. If the side panel is closed, neither message is ever sent, and the badge count stays elevated permanently.

Fix in `platform/browser-extension/src/confirmation-badge.ts`:
- Add a `Map<string, ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>` for background-side confirmation timeouts
- In `notifyConfirmationRequest`, extract the `timeoutMs` from params (`params.timeoutMs` is a number) and set a background timeout = `timeoutMs + 2000` (slightly longer than the server timeout + the side panel's +1000 buffer). When it fires, call `clearConfirmationBadge()` and delete the map entry.
- Export a new `clearConfirmationBackgroundTimeout(id: string)` function that clears a specific timeout (called when the side panel handles it first).
- In `clearAllConfirmationBadges`, also clear all background timeouts.

Then in `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts`:
- Import `clearConfirmationBackgroundTimeout` from confirmation-badge.ts
- In `handleSpConfirmationResponse` (line 162-172), call `clearConfirmationBackgroundTimeout(message.data.id)` (extract the id from message.data)
- In `handleSpConfirmationTimeout` (line 175-178), call `clearConfirmationBackgroundTimeout(message.id)` (the id is on the message)

Also update `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` in the `confirmation.request` handler (line 450-452): pass the full params to `notifyConfirmationRequest` — it already does this. Verify that `params.timeoutMs` is present; check the MCP server's confirmation request payload.

The `notifyConfirmationRequest` function already receives `params: Record<string, unknown>`, so accessing `params.timeoutMs` and `params.id` is straightforward with typeof checks.
